_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 7-30 Active Compression-Decompression Device (Revised 6/27/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan Procedures Protocol Section 7-30: Active Compression-Decompression Device — MDHHS Approved 6/27/2023
purpose: MCA OPTIONAL. The Active Compression-Decompression (ACD) device provides both active compression AND active decompression of the chest during CPR, enhancing venous return. MUST be used in conjunction with the Impedance Threshold Device (ITD). MCAs are responsible for provider training and submitting training documentation to MDHHS.

sections.title: Authorization and Training
points: MCA OPTIONAL — only authorized if MCA has approved ACD device use
MCAs are responsible for: provider training AND submission of training documentation to MDHHS
All providers using the ACD device must be trained per MCA requirements

sections.title: Indication and Required Pairing
points: REQUIRED PAIRING: ACD device MUST be used in conjunction with the Impedance Threshold Device (ITD) — refer to Section 7-11 ITD Protocol
The ACD device and ITD work synergistically — the ACD creates active decompression that works with the ITD to enhance venous return during CPR decompression phase

sections.title: Discontinuation
points: RETURN OF SPONTANEOUS CIRCULATION (ROSC): discontinue ACD device IMMEDIATELY upon ROSC
Manage patient per ROSC Protocol after discontinuation

sections.title: Documentation
points: Document ACD device use in PCR
Document time of initiation and discontinuation
Document patient outcome relative to ACD use (ROSC achieved, continued arrest, TOR)

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must select whether an ACD device is authorized for use in cardiac arrest
options: Authorized — FDA-approved ACD device; MCA-required training completed; training submitted to MDHHS

deterioration_flags: ROSC achieved — STOP ACD device IMMEDIATELY; manage per ROSC Protocol
ITD not available — do NOT use ACD device alone; ACD requires concurrent ITD use; use standard manual CPR
scope_notes: MCA OPTIONAL — only authorized if MCA has approved and submitted training to MDHHS
MUST be used in conjunction with ITD (Section 7-11)
MCAs responsible for training and MDHHS training submission
Document in PCR: device use, times, concurrent ITD use, patient outcome
